opp.name: Publicis - CLM and Request Management
opp.next_step: 29th of June - Confidence : 90% Good call with Émeline she did not came back on Merlin for I Contract to be put as option instead of core module which would have a significant impact (50K if we consider discount) on TCV (let's see if Florence will insist or not) we are almost there for OF1, Advanced support options to be added (good news potential additional revenue ! ) and 2 minor typos / format to be changed. If we can have this by tomorrow that would be great. For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July. 24th of June - Confidence : 80% BAFO sent today Negotiation meeting scheduled on 25th of June Second round of legal comments should be received from Publicis by 25th of June Questionnaire to finalize SoW / OF2 should be received by 25th of June => Close plan - Final version for final review of contractual package to be sent by Zycus on 29th / 30th of June => Comments / last negotiation calls from 1st of July till 10th of July => Signature target date : 15th of July => Project start last week of July 12th of June - Confidence : 80% Publicis : Positive => Émeline (purchaser) super pushy - already 4 version of OF1 - we included IAnalyze as option could significantly increase the deal size / Supplier creation process launched - Legal : OK were supposed to send first remark this week will be more probably early next week (Émeline is off, she will be back Monday) so not perfectly on time but ok for legal Questionnable (not negative at this stage) => Willingness of Stephen Cook (Head of procurement performance (so in charge of processes and tools) and Paras (external consultant important in tech validation) to move forward as I sent 3 reminders asking them to schedule a WS to finalize contract / John one => 0 Feedback. Action : I already sent them a 4th reminder with questionnaire from Hrishikesh for them to prepare this WS if no answer by Monday afternoon we need to escalate (direct call to Florence, ask for a call with Aatish...others.. to be discussed) 4st of june - Confidence : 80% We answered with our completed OF1 to 3 points. NS and agreed close plan 5th of june : send a new OF1 to answer to Emeline requests to reduce the budget for FY26 - We should have an acceptable answer Starting 8th of June : Start legal discusssion on MSA (no clear close plan / no contact with Legal at this stage) 8th of June : Publicis following our presentation on 2nd of June should come back to us with additional questions to ensure that the high level vision of our champion Florence is really aligned with their contractual processes (that's the main risk) Between 8th nd 12th of June : Schedule a 90 minutes WS to be able to create a SoW (OF2) to be sent around 15th of June Joined target shared by Publicis and Zycus : Start the project in July for a go live (quick win) before end of FY26 => Need to finalize agreement end of June / First week of July (at this stage I let our close date in July) => Secure this close plan with a Top Management Call Aatish and Florence / Need first to have their questions and have a good knowledge about potential blockers so this call will not be before 15th of June. 1st of June - Confidence : 80% Starting Contractual discussion at the end of our call today. 4 points to be addressed. Still need to Come back with OF1 completed asap (with clear answer on 4 points) Schedule a SoW WS in the coming days / Prior to this Publicis to come back with additional questions that could oblige us to make a second presentations based on their uses cases - Close plan not entirely clear depending of the list of questions we will receive in the coming days / Florence as champion confirmed her willingness to close asap and have a quick win (solution in production) before end of 26. => See how we will have to involve Aatish for a call with Florence to ensure our full support (at this stage I think we need first to have their feedback so additional questions and us to send OF1 before to have an efficient call with clear take away). 26th May - Vendor Selected 21st of May - Confidence : 80% Next steps - Shared with Publicis Contract In order to send you two order forms - one for the subscription, OF1, and one for the implementation, OF2, we would need to organize a short 90-minute workshop with your technical team. The objective would be to validate all assumptions and ensure that OF2 is fully aligned with Publicis’ expectations. This workshop can be organized as soon as possible. Once it has taken place, we will need four working days to finalize OF1 and OF2 and send them to you for review. A possible retro-planning could therefore be: Workshop next week OF1 and OF2 sent by 5 June for review Target to finalize the agreement before the end of June Project start in July Go-live before the end of 2026 11th of May - Confidence : 40% Assumptions for pricing sent / Need confirmation from CPO (Florence) + input on number of existing contracts PS Estimation needs to be done by 15th of may Proposal to be sent on 15th of May / WS to refine implementation time line and assumptions to be scheduled end of May. First version of OF1 / OF2 mid of June / Negotiation process : Target date signature mid of Juy.
opp.ais_why:
• SF Description identifies Florence as 'a strong CPO champion' from PwC where Zycus deployed ‚Äî a named CPO who has prior Zycus experience and is now driving the Publicis evaluation. Product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake within the CLM/iRequest stack (iContract, iRequest, iSupplier, Merlin, Merlin Intake). The differentiation narrative names AI-driven sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and automated compliance.
• Qualified stage with proposal sent 1 May 2026 and target signature mid-July. 4 prior Avoma meetings (May/Sep 2025) with Cedric Elmaleh predate Florence's CPO arrival and are not directly relevant. The current cycle is partner-led (Bertrand intro) with no recorded Florence call yet on this specific opp.
• Florence's compelling event per Description is a year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact, not specifically AI; the deal is structured as a quick-win CLM/Intake replication of the PwC blueprint. Pushed UP from prior 5 to 6 based on the rich Description + Merlin in scope + named CPO champion with prior Zycus experience. Score 6 reflects Florence's potential AI championship without explicit AI-led mandate captured yet.

items.quote: Pain stated qualitatively ('contractual process is a mess'), never converted to cost, cycle-time or savings figures

Only Avoma record is the 2025-09-30 Paris dinner event (Cedric Elmaleh), predating Florence's CPO arrival; the current cycle is evidenced by SF Next Step and the email/activity thread, not recorded calls

sources: SF pain fields / MEDDPICC 2.0 Identify pain
metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated clear qualitative operational pain but no quantified value case has been captured. The pain is process inefficiency: manual email-based contract validation, no dashboard, and no easy visibility into contract status or next steps (SF pain fields, Next Step 2026-06-04). Florence's mandate is a year-end push to demonstrate procurement impact with a quick-win go-live before end of FY26 (AIS_Why, Next Step 2026-06-01), which implies urgency but not a dollar figure. No contract cycle-time, headcount, or compliance-cost number has been stated. Quantifying this in an ROI workshop with Publicis procurement/finance and anchoring the $250K to a savings and speed number remains the open move.

champion.narrative: There are two champions. Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, Decision Maker) is the strategic champion: she selected Zycus sole-source, has prior Zycus deployment experience from PwC, and set the quick-win urgency. Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director) is the operational champion who drives the deal daily - 'super pushy', ran 4+ OF1 iterations, handled the BAFO, and pulls Zycus for calls (requested Friday/Monday call, 2026-07-01). What has strengthened since last sweep: the internal blockers cleared (legal resolved, functional doc delivered, Stephen Cook and Paras re-engaged for the 2026-07-06 workshop). What is still missing: Florence's explicit sign-off on the final commercial package and on keeping Merlin as core rather than an option.

competition.narrative: There is no external competitor: Publicis selected Zycus sole-source with no RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence's PwC track record. The only competitive pressure is internal. First, status-quo / do-nothing risk, which is low given the stated pain and Florence's year-end quick-win mandate. Second, and more current, internal de-scoping: Emeline is pushing to move Merlin for iContract from core module to an option, a roughly $50K TCV swing (Next Step 2026-06-29), which also erodes the AI differentiation. We win by keeping Merlin core, tying its contract-intelligence and agentic-intake value to the confirmed pain (visibility, process mess) and the quick-win go-live, and getting Florence to bless the scope.

sources: SF pain field / MEDDPICC 2.0 Identify pain
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a broken manual contract-management process owned by procurement: 'Actual contractual process is a mess / Email validation / No dashboard / No possibility to know easily what are the next steps' (SF pain field / Next Step 2026-06-04). The remedy scoped is iContract for centralised CLM, iRequest for request/approval automation, and Merlin for contract intelligence and visibility. The pain owner is Florence (CPO) and her procurement team, and her year-end mandate to show impact with a quick win before end of FY26 gives the pain a clock. It is confirmed but qualitative; the financial magnitude has not been quantified.

paper_process.narrative: Paperwork is well advanced. The MSA had all legal clauses resolved with Exhibit 2 (VOC process) updated and handed back for final review (2026-06-29/30); the NDA is fully executed (Adobe Sign, 2026-06-29); OF1 (subscription order form) is near-final with only minor typos outstanding (Next Step 2026-06-29). OF2 (implementation SoW) is the remaining artifact, to be built from the 2026-07-06 functional workshop. Vendor compliance runs through Publicis Re:Sources: the anti-bribery / PASS self-assessment was signed 2026-06-17 and completed 2026-07-02, and Ariba supplier-network onboarding is in progress, but the OneTrust vendor security declaration is only 6 of 17 questions answered (Praveen, 2026-06-30) with access issues for Zycus legal still being resolved. The forcing function is the buyer's own 2026-07-15 signature target and late-July go-live.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, Decision Maker, PRIMARY
Email 2026-06-24 Updated Commercial Proposal to Florence and Emeline
Email 2026-06-24 John Woodcock (VP EMEA) catch-up with Florence
economic_buyer.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, is the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker (OpportunityContactRole, PRIMARY, Role=Decision Maker) and the named budget owner. She made the sole-source vendor selection and carries a year-end mandate to show procurement impact. Her authority showed when the Updated Commercial Proposal was addressed directly to her (2026-06-24) and Zycus VP EMEA John Woodcock arranged to catch up with her the next day ('Looking forward to catching up tomorrow', 2026-06-24). Day-to-day commercial and legal negotiation is run by Emeline Le Foyer (procurement), but final sign-off authority sits with the relevant stakeholder direct footprint on execution detail is light, so confirming her explicit sign-off on the final package and on Merlin scope is the priority.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a partner-introduced (Bertrand), sole-source vendor selection that moved to the relevant stakeholder on 2026-05-26 and is now in structured contract execution. Read from the activity sequence: OF1 iterated through 4+ versions with Emeline, BAFO sent 2026-06-24, MSA legal resolved 2026-06-29/30, NDA executed 2026-06-29, functional SoW questionnaire returned 2026-07-01, and a finalisation review call set for 2026-07-06 with Stephen Cook (process validation) and Paras (technical validation). The approval chain runs Emeline Le Foyer (procurement lead) and Publicis Re:Sources (Praveen Seemundun, supplier onboarding and compliance) up to Florence for final sign-off, with legal on both sides. Final negotiation is planned 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-10 ahead of a 2026-07-15 signature and late-July project start.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is anchored on replicating the proven PwC CLM/Intake blueprint where Florence previously deployed Zycus, delivered as a quick win in production before end of FY26. Concrete criteria surfaced during contract execution: functional fit validated via the functional questionnaire Publicis returned (2026-07-01), the support model and what it includes (support model proposals shared 2026-06-22), travel-expense clarity for budgeting (prep call 2026-06-18), and whether Merlin for iContract is scoped as core or an option (Next Step 2026-06-29). Fit is being validated against Publicis' contractual processes, which the rep flagged as 'the main risk' that the high-level champion vision aligns to operational reality (Next Step 2026-06-01).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're well-positioned — the real risk is timing and sign-off, not a competitor.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Vendor Selected' anchors near 72. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 92.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Two-tier champion structure, strengthening this sweep. Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, Decision Maker) is the strategic champion who selected Zycus sole-source on her PwC track record and set the FY26 quick-win mandate; Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director) is the operational champion driving daily execution, negotiation, and OF1 iterations, and pulls Zycus for calls. Since last sweep the internal blockers that flagged champion risk cleared: legal resolved, functional doc delivered, and Stephen Cook and Paras re-engaged for the 2026-07-06 workshop. What remains is Florence's explicit sign-off on the final commercial package and on keeping Merlin core.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Two-tier champion structure, strengthening this sweep.

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Sole-source: Publicis selected Zycus without an RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence's prior PwC deployment, so there is no external competitor to displace.

bullets.text: FY26 budget pressure pushing Merlin for iContract from core to option, roughly $50K TCV erosion, unresolved.
OneTrust vendor security declaration only 6 of 17 questions answered, with Zycus legal still lacking OneTrust access - a live gate to the 2026-07-15 signature.
OF2/SoW is not yet built with 12 days to signature; it depends on the 2026-07-06 workshop landing cleanly to leave room for final negotiation (1-10 July).

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.
blocks.title: Customer commitment

items: Have John Woodcock confirm with Florence that Merlin for iContract stays core. (by 8 Jul)
Finalise OF2/SoW scope with Stephen Cook and Paras on the 6 July review call. (by 6 Jul)
Complete and submit the Publicis OneTrust vendor security declaration. (by 7 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Publicis Groupe — Vendor Selected · $250K · closes 15 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 92 is carried by preference strength +1.00 (weight 20); champion strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 89 is anchored on the stage win (92), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6.0 (Moderate). Merlin, Merlin Intake and Agentic AI are in the product scope and the CPO champion has prior Zycus deployment experience, signalling genuine AI appetite, but the buyer's compelling event is a year-end procurement-impact mandate rather than an explicit AI mandate, and Emeline is pushing to make Merlin optional. AI interest is real but not yet decisive - AI Curious rather than AI Hungry.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed ('contractual process is a mess / no dashboard / no visibility') and a FY26 quick-win mandate, but no quantified ROI, savings, or cycle-time figures captured.

Publicis returned near-executable revised legal document (second round of comments)
Emeline Le Foyer sent the completed functional SoW document
Finalisation review call scheduled for Monday 2026-07-06 with Stephen Cook and Paras preparing (previously stalled workshop unblocked)

Amount raised 230,000 to 250,000 and forecast moved Best Case to Commit

deal_movement.summary: Strong forward movement this sweep as the deal moves through final contract execution. The three dependencies that put the date at risk last read have largely cleared: MSA legal resolved, functional SoW questionnaire received, and the stalled workshop with Stephen Cook and Paras booked for 2026-07-06. Buyer engagement is daily and two-way through 2026-07-02. Remaining: build OF2/SoW, resolve Merlin scope, complete the security declaration.

items.text: The SoW finalisation review with Stephen Cook and Paras, stalled for weeks, is finally booked for 6 July, unblocking the last artifact before signature.
All MSA legal clauses are resolved and the NDA is signed, but the OneTrust vendor security declaration is only a third complete with 12 days to the 15 July signature.
This is a sole-source selection with no rival; the only value threat is the buyer's push to make Merlin for iContract optional, a roughly 50,000 hit to deal size.
Praveen Seemundun (Category Manager, IT Services at Publicis Re:Sources) is now actively driving supplier onboarding and the compliance questionnaire, an engaged procurement-operations thread.
The buyer asked to add advanced support options and wants travel-expense clarity to budget, a potential additional-revenue lever to lock in the final order form.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and hot: daily two-way email through 2026-07-02, buyer setting the pace ('super pushy'), NDA signed, legal resolved, and a finalisation call booked for 2026-07-06. No cooling signals.

north_star_verdict.math: 12 days to close (2026-07-15, today 2026-07-03). Opp created 2026-05-07; Vendor Selected reached 2026-05-26 (38 days in stage, normal for enterprise contract execution). Buyer's own signature target is 2026-07-15 and both sides remain aligned ('target is still mid of July', Next Step 2026-06-29). Prior three overdue dependencies have largely cleared this sweep: (a) MSA legal - 'All legal clauses have now been resolved' (2026-06-29/30) and Publicis returned a near-executable revised document (2026-06-26); (b) SoW functional questionnaire - Emeline sent the completed functional document (2026-07-01); (c) the SoW workshop stalled 19 days on Stephen Cook and Paras is now a scheduled review call Monday 2026-07-06 with both preparing. Remaining before signature: build and finalise OF2/SoW off Monday's call, resolve Merlin core-vs-option ($50K TCV), complete the OneTrust vendor security declaration (6 of 17 answered as of 2026-06-30), and run final negotiation (planned 2026-07-01 to 2026-07-10). CloseDate nudged 2026-07-13 to 2026-07-15 on 2026-06-29 (minor). The date is aggressive but plausible; if OF2 finalisation or the security declaration slips past 2026-07-08 the signature slips past 2026-07-15.
evidence: Next Step 2026-06-29 (Confidence 90%): 'Good call with Emeline ... we are almost there for OF1, Advanced support options to be added (good news potential additional revenue!) and 2 minor typos / format to be changed ... For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July.'
Email 2026-06-29/30: 'All legal clauses have now been resolved, and I have also updated Exhibit 2 related to the VOC process. I will let you perform a final review.' MSA effectively closed.
Email 2026-06-26 (from Emeline Le Foyer): 'Please find attached our revised document ... to get closer to executable version.' Second round of legal comments received.

Email 2026-07-01 (from Emeline Le Foyer): 'Please find attached the document completed on the functional part ... Do you have availability on Friday or Monday to discuss it?' SoW functional questionnaire received.
Task 2026-07-01: Confirmation call Monday 2026-07-06 at CET being booked; 'allow Stephen and Paras to prepare' - the previously stalled SoW workshop is unblocked.
NDA fully executed 2026-06-29 (Adobe Sign, all parties finished).
LastActivityDate 2026-07-06; two-way email cadence daily through 2026-07-02 (buyer highly engaged).
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy sole-source deal now clearing its blockers: MSA legal fully resolved, NDA signed, functional doc received and the stalled SoW workshop finally booked for 2026-07-06, but OF2/SoW is unbuilt and the OneTrust security review incomplete with only 12 days to the 2026-07-15 signature.

flags: Protect $50K of TCV: get Florence to explicitly confirm Merlin for iContract stays core before OF1 is frozen, framing it as the enabler of the FY26 quick win, not an optional add-on.

Clear the security gate now: the OneTrust vendor declaration (6 of 17 answered) and Zycus legal access are the most likely late blockers to a 2026-07-15 signature; complete them ahead of the contract package.
Use the 2026-07-06 review to lock OF2/SoW in one pass with Stephen Cook (process) and Paras (technical) so the final negotiation window (1-10 July) is not consumed rebuilding scope.
best_practice_check.summary: Late-stage contract-execution best practices for a sole-source deal 12 days from signature: protect deal value on Merlin scope, close the security gate, and convert champion urgency into a signed executable package.

customer_preference.evidence: Publicis selected Zycus sole-source with no RFP, requested the updated commercial proposal, and is 'super pushy' to close by mid-July ('we are almost there for OF1 ... target is still mid of July').

competitive_position.summary: Sole-source: Publicis selected Zycus without an RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence's prior PwC deployment, so there is no external competitor to displace. The current threat is internal, not a rival: (1) de-scoping Merlin for iContract from core to an option, a roughly $50K TCV erosion under active negotiation (highest current value threat, 2026-06-29); and (2) status-quo / manual-process inertia, which is low given the confirmed pain and Florence's year-end quick-win mandate. No new external competitor surfaced this sweep. We protect value by keeping Merlin core, anchored to the contract-visibility pain and the FY26 go-live.

competitors.how_we_win: Keep Merlin as core by tying its contract-intelligence and agentic-intake value to the stated pain (visibility, process mess) and the quick-win go-live; secure Florence's explicit sign-off before OF1 is frozen.

competitors.how_we_win: Centralise CLM (iContract), automate request/approval (iRequest), and deliver contract intelligence via Merlin; Florence's FY26 quick-win urgency keeps do-nothing risk low.

The Merlin/agentic-intake narrative needs to be landed with Florence as the enabler of the quick win, not a nice-to-have.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit against the buyer's stated expectations: a quick-win CLM/Intake go-live before end of FY26 replicating the proven PwC blueprint, with legal and functional now largely aligned. The one exposed area is the AI/Merlin scope, where FY26 budget pressure is pushing Merlin toward optional and could dilute the differentiation.
